The classification of Abama ossifraga places it within the order Malpighiales, a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Malpighiales order is further divided into several families, with the Euphorbiaceae family being one of the largest and most diverse. Abama ossifraga is a relatively small genus within the Euphorbiaceae family, with a limited number of species. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of Abama ossifraga within the plant kingdom.

Its therapeutic properties are believed to include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant's roots and leaves are often used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions.
